purpose: Structured inventory for the Mathematica Codex Helper resource-aware scaffold. Entries are candidate Wolfram resources to verify before use, not guarantees of live availability.

modes.name: docs-only
modes.description: Use static SKILL.md and references guidance when live Wolfram resources are unavailable or unnecessary.

modes.name: resource-aware
modes.description: Resolve named Wolfram prompts or example resources when package usage, code transformation, or verified examples materially affect correctness.

modes.name: strict-validation
modes.description: Add an assessment pass, evidence card, smoke test, and mathematical or physical sanity check for high-risk answers.

prompts.name: WolframSampleCode
prompts.role: canonical short usage examples for Wolfram Language functions and resources
prompts.use_when: A user asks how to use a Wolfram symbol, ResourceFunction, or concise language pattern.

prompts.name: CodeReformat
prompts.role: post-generation style cleanup for Wolfram Language code
prompts.use_when: User asks for code readability, formatting, or cleanup without changing semantics.

prompts.name: CodeCommentInsert
prompts.role: intent-focused comments for Wolfram Language code
prompts.use_when: User asks to add comments to existing Mathematica code.

prompts.name: CodeDocAnnotator
prompts.role: package documentation scaffolding and usage-message drafting
prompts.use_when: User asks for usage messages, function docs, or paclet-style documentation text.

prompts.name: FunctionNameSuggest
prompts.role: readable Wolfram Language function naming suggestions
prompts.use_when: User asks to rename functions or improve package API readability.

prompts.name: VariableNameSuggest
prompts.use_when: User asks to clarify notebook or package code variable names.

prompts.name: LLMPromptAssessment
prompts.role: self-assessment rubric for hallucination, convention, and evidence checks
prompts.use_when: Strict validation is requested or the answer uses unfamiliar Wolfram package APIs.

examples.name: Generate Email Replies with a Semantic Search Memory
examples.role: pattern for maintaining a compact trusted example memory
examples.use_when: Future work adds a local cache or retrieval memory of verified Wolfram examples.

examples.name: Provide Socioeconomic Data with an LLM Tool to Avoid Hallucinations
examples.role: pattern for tool-mediated exact lookup to avoid hallucinated facts or symbols
examples.use_when: Future work exposes a Wolfram evidence lookup helper.

examples.name: Re-ranking Text Matches in Semantic Search Queries
examples.role: pattern for reranking retrieved snippets before adapting them
examples.use_when: Future work adds semantic search over verified manual or example snippets.

strict_mode_requires: named prompt or example candidates when relevant
manual or example evidence before unfamiliar package APIs
one syntax, load, or static validation check
one mathematical or physical sanity check for scientific workflows
explicit note when live Wolfram resources were not available
fail_closed_behavior: do not invent prompt names, example names, context strings, option names, or package symbols
fall back to docs-only mode when resources cannot be verified
state uncertainty in the final answer rather than hiding it
